# CI Housekeeping

Enforce conventions across CI tooling, skill generation, and GitHub workflow generation.

## Scope

- `packages/ci/skill-gen/` — the `@ci/skill-gen` generator (`scan`, `parse`, `merge`, `output`)
- `packages/ci/workflow-gen/` — the `@ci/workflow-gen` generator (`scan`, YAML `parse`, `merge`, `output`)
- `packages/ci/actions/` — co-located CI check packages that contribute `//<workflow-gen>` fragments
- Generated `.agents/skills/*/SKILL.md` files (those carrying the `<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by skill-gen -- DO NOT EDIT -->` header)
- Generated `.github/workflows/*.yml` files (those carrying the `# AUTO-GENERATED by workflow-gen -- DO NOT EDIT` header)
- `required-workflows.json` generated from workflows marked `required: true`

## Source of Truth

`packages/ci/skill-gen/SPEC.md`, `packages/ci/workflow-gen/SPEC.md`, and `AGENTS.md` conventions.

## What to Check

| Check | Details |
|-------|---------|
| skill-gen freshness | `bun run skill-gen:check` passes (committed skills match their source fragments) |
| workflow-gen freshness | `bun run workflow-gen:check` passes (committed workflows and `required-workflows.json` match source fragments) |
| Marker validity | All `//<skill-gen>` / `//</skill-gen>` markers in source are well-formed and balanced (no nested or unclosed blocks) |
| Workflow marker validity | All `//<workflow-gen>` / `//</workflow-gen>` markers in source are well-formed and balanced |
| Frontmatter completeness | Every fragment has the required `name` and `description` keys |
| Generated file integrity | Auto-generated SKILL.md files keep the `AUTO-GENERATED` header and have no manual edits |
| Workflow integrity | Auto-generated workflow files keep the `AUTO-GENERATED` header and have no manual edits |
| Template variables | `$$file` and `$$directory` expand correctly in fragments |
| Effect patterns | generator source (`packages/ci/{skill-gen,workflow-gen}/src/**`) follows the conventions in `domain-effect` |

## Discovery

```bash
# Source files carrying skill fragments
grep -rl "//<skill-gen>" --include="*.ts" packages

# Source files carrying workflow fragments
grep -rl "//<workflow-gen>" --include="*.ts" packages

# Generated skill files
grep -rl "AUTO-GENERATED by skill-gen" .agents/skills

# Generated workflow files
grep -rl "AUTO-GENERATED by workflow-gen" .github/workflows
```

## Audit Approach

1. Run `bun run skill-gen:check` and `bun run workflow-gen:check` to detect drift. If stale, fix the source fragments and regenerate — never edit generated `SKILL.md` or workflow YAML directly.
2. Scan source files for `//<skill-gen>` and `//<workflow-gen>` markers; verify they are balanced and each fragment has the required fields.
3. Verify auto-generated files still carry the header and were not hand-edited.
4. Check `packages/ci/{skill-gen,workflow-gen}/src/**` against `domain-effect` conventions and `packages/ci/actions/**` against `domain-cli` conventions.
